# Why most landing pages quietly waste 60% of their click traffic

Most teams ship a hero, ship a CTA, and call it a day. The data tells a different story.

What if the bottleneck is not the hero, but the second scroll?

Here is what we measured across 12 SaaS sites over the last quarter:

- 3x lift in qualified sign-ups after rewriting the second scroll
- 18% bounce reduction once the CTA moved below the proof section
- A clear pattern: visitors who scroll past 60% convert 4x more often than those who do not

The pattern is consistent enough to be boring. Audit your second scroll, move social proof above the fold of the second screen, and only then ask the reader to subscribe.
